[2018-05-29 12:16] leary93: @Gorm on the topic of learning strategies: its very counterproductive to say you got beat by shitty strategies or that a set is base set anyways. That way, instead of learning why bonus units worked against you, you push it off. If you get beat by a "shitty" strategy than u prob played an even more shit strategy, and if you call a set basically bso, you dont learn to identify why units work. Even in bso+1, with a nonbso viable unit, that doesnt make the set basically bso, because maybe the reason that unit isnt viable is because it would just change how the bso beats it (like say hypothetically cryo ray isnt bso viable because it gets beat by gauss spam, then it isnt basically bso, because bso is basically a relatively low econ tarsier/wall mix)

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-05-29) ---
[2018-05-29 18:51] scythius: I'm relatively new and still tier 5, so I was just wondering in which case would going for Shadowfang be better
[2018-05-29 18:52] amalloy: if green is bad; if it's convenient to get a third red
[2018-05-29 18:52] p0lari (Polari): high econ set where you can conveniently get the third red?
[2018-05-29 18:52] scythius: basically, a mobile animus, flame animus or a centrifuge?
[2018-05-29 18:52] swapgo: or mid econ games where there's fixing
[2018-05-29 18:52] p0lari (Polari): like you have manimus or flanimus or can dump the fourth red into cybes or phage or whatever
[2018-05-29 18:52] swapgo: Mobile Animus, Flame Animus or Chrono Filter
[2018-05-29 18:52] swapgo: Maybe big econ games when there's a good sink
[2018-05-29 18:53] swapgo: Like Blood Phage, Corprus or w/e
[2018-05-29 18:53] scythius: I'm still trying to get a sense of the game's balance, it feels fairly balanced, but I still feel that units like Militia are slightly underpowered
[2018-05-29 18:53] swapgo: Militia is really really good for two things
[2018-05-29 18:53] amalloy: it's easier to mass-produce fangs than to mass-produce cynestras
[2018-05-29 18:53] swapgo: One, Breachproof, and two, Iso Kronus / Sync attackers
[2018-05-29 18:53] p0lari (Polari): usually getting a couple of cynestra is super good but fang is much better at an "I'm going to get most of my attack from this unit"
[2018-05-29 18:54] p0lari (Polari): what amalloy said yeah
[2018-05-29 18:54] amalloy: @SwapGo also, denying absorb when building it early
[2018-05-29 18:54] swapgo: It's a better first attacker than S because if your opponent runs out absorb you can get money
[2018-05-29 18:54] p0lari (Polari): like if you're mid econ with one conduit and get a cynestra as first attacker and  a second later that's great
[2018-05-29 18:54] scythius: so, militia shouldn't be rushed generally speaking?
[2018-05-29 18:55] swapgo: It's a good first attacker, but you will lose massing them
[2018-05-29 18:55] amalloy: @Scythius "rushed" is different from "be your first attacker"
[2018-05-29 18:55] swapgo: If you go DD/DD/DDB then sure why not Militia
[2018-05-29 18:55] p0lari (Polari): if you buy out drones in a defensive set and want to start pumping out attack, do you get triple conduit animus and buy four cynestra?
[2018-05-29 18:55] scythius: triple conduit is a big tech investment I guess
[2018-05-29 18:56] scythius: but I don't expect to pump out Cynestras every turn
[2018-05-29 18:56] p0lari (Polari): I mean, maybe? depending what else is in set
[2018-05-29 18:56] p0lari (Polari): but if you have any of the many different red fixers, setting up fang and buying one every turn is so much easier in that scenario
[2018-05-29 18:56] amalloy: right, that's where shadowfang can be better, because if you have the tech for one you have the tech (and gold) to build one every turn
[2018-05-29 18:57] scythius: I've never rated shadowfang highly before
[2018-05-29 18:57] amalloy: well, it's usually not playable
[2018-05-29 18:57] scythius: maybe I should try it more often if there's good red support in the set
[2018-05-29 18:57] p0lari (Polari): it's like the second most efficient attacker in game or something?
[2018-05-29 18:57] scythius: Which attackers are the most efficient in general?
[2018-05-29 18:58] shadourow (Rando): Lucina is the first, and Shadowfang the 2nd, (I'm not sure, but it's probably true)
[2018-05-29 18:59] amalloy: what does p1 do in a set like +D400-D1Byr? i can never figure out how to cope with tia threat when everything in the advanced set is red/green.
[2018-05-29 18:59] leary93: Depends, though, both lancetooth and bloodrager can be quite strong when denying absorb
[2018-05-29 18:59] amalloy: @Giselle pls. +D400-D1Byr without the question mark?
[2018-05-29 18:59] leary93: Well, if u cant cope, do it yourself
[2018-05-29 19:00] amalloy: well, i did. but a naked tia is so weak
[2018-05-29 19:00] p0lari (Polari): not sure what else to even consider
[2018-05-29 19:00] leary93: Time them together and u get a crazy burst
[2018-05-29 19:01] amalloy: yes. scorch into tia is what i tried to do, but i couldn't get the details right
[2018-05-29 19:01] leary93: In that case, just fiddle around a bit
[2018-05-29 19:01] leary93: See what lines work bettee
[2018-05-29 19:02] p0lari (Polari): chrono filter WutFace

[2018-05-30 22:14] liadahlia (Lia): i just hate it because it makes math harder
[2018-05-30 22:19] amalloy: does it? why? i think the math is just as easy with a bunch of pixies lying around. say i have 2 xeno guardians and i want to lose neither. if the opponent is threatening 8, i need to defend for 8+4+1 to keep them both alive. if they're threatening 25, i need to defend for 25+4+1. neither of those seems any harder, because there's only one big number at a time
[2018-05-30 22:19] amalloy: whereas like, having 4 xenos or 7 patrollers or something does seem harder to me
[2018-05-30 22:19] liadahlia (Lia): like mrguy, i can only count up to 10
[2018-05-30 22:19] liadahlia (Lia): numbers like 31 makes no sense to me

[2018-05-30 22:20] amalloy: then 10 is a larger number

[2018-05-30 22:20] liadahlia (Lia): because i can't tell which defense is gonna die

[2018-06-02 23:40] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): Cuz even if they can buy urbans in response to bites, you still force them to buy urbans, which may or may not be convenient for them
[2018-06-02 23:48] 1m2o: bite is always decent
[2018-06-02 23:49] 1m2o: Any tips on redemer btw i'm losing every single game with this card i don't have idea what to do vs it and if i have to go for it
[2018-06-02 23:49] 307th: mostly treat it like a regular attacker
[2018-06-02 23:50] 307th: the main exception is that you are vulnerable to burst damage on the turn the charges come in
[2018-06-02 23:50] 307th: or, if your opponent goes for it, they're vulnerable
[2018-06-02 23:50] 307th: so, redeemer tends to get stronger when good soak is in the set (since you can block the charges with e.g. aegis), but weaker if there's good burst in the set like Nivo Charge
[2018-06-02 23:50] 307th: when playing against it, you won't always be able to take advantage of the gauss charge turn, but try to at least have some damage of your own
[2018-06-02 23:51] 307th: so that they don't get to just absorb the gauss charges
